name: Great hall
description: Large ceremonial throne hall: a tall, long chamber with a raised dais and throne centered on the far short wall, two rows of pillars framing a long central carpet, flanking braziers, hanging lantern chandeliers, and ceremonial banners on the long walls.

required_furniture.rule: A single @stairs block centered on the dais against the far short wall, facing into the room toward the entrance.
A 2-3 block raised platform of @accent under the throne, 2-3 wide × 2 deep, flush against the far short wall.
Two rows of square @primary pillars running the full hall height along the long axis, framing the central carpet path — 4-6 pillars per row when the AABB allows.
A long @carpet runner one cell above the floor, running from the entrance short wall to the foot of the dais. 2 cells wide when the hall width allows (>= 10), else 1.
minecraft:campfire blocks (or minecraft:fire on a minecraft:netherrack base when there is no headroom) flanking the carpet runner, distributed evenly along the long axis.
minecraft:lantern[hanging=true] suspended one cell below the conceptual ceiling row, aligned with the central path.
minecraft:red_carpet blocks affixed to the interior face of the long walls in the upper third of the hall, staggered along the long axis as ceremonial heraldry.

placement_rules: Dais sits flush against the throne wall, never blocking the carpet
Braziers flank the carpet, never overlap it
Chandelier lanterns hang above the central path
Banners decorate the long walls only, never the short walls
lighting_ventilation: Vertical lighting layered top-to-bottom: hanging lantern chandeliers along the central axis at the high level, braziers (campfire or fire-on-netherrack) at floor level along the carpet. Tall hall (≥ 4 high) and the open lid (no solid ceiling) keep smoke and verticality legible.

medieval.notes: Heavy stone throne hall, red ceremonial banners, iron lanterns, campfire braziers as cresset stand-ins.

modern.notes: Sleek civic chamber: quartz columns, gray carpet runner, recessed lantern fittings replace open fire braziers when possible.

fantasy.notes: Dark-oak pillared hall with purpur dais, purple-carpet runner, sea-lantern chandeliers; banners read as enchanted heraldry.
